query: PostgreSQL TimescaleDB real-time analytics time series 2024
answer: TimescaleDB enhances PostgreSQL for real-time analytics on time-series data, offering hypertables for efficient partitioning and querying. It uses a hybrid row/columnar engine for fast data scans. TimescaleDB supports scalable, high-performance analytics.

results.title: Setting Up TimescaleDB on Postgres.app for Time-Series Data
results.snippet: # Setting Up TimescaleDB for Time Series Data with Postgres.app for all your “Time Scales”! TimescaleDB solves this by turning your time-series tables into hypertables that automatically partition by time, compress old data, and optimize queries. Postgres.app offers TimescaleDB as a downloadable extension installer. Head over to the Postgres.app Extensions page and grab the **TimescaleDB package for PostgreSQL 18**. The installer shows you’re installing timescaledb 2.23.1 for PostgreSQL 18 and handles everything: it drops the extension files into `~/Library/Application Support/Postgres/Extensions/18/` and configures PostgreSQL 18 to find them. TimescaleDB’s hypertables automatically partition data into time-based chunks. But for local development with Postgres.app on PostgreSQL 18, the downloadable extension installer makes it painless—just download, double-click, restart, and you’re ready to go. ## And That’s It. You now have TimescaleDB running on Postgres.app with PostgreSQL 18. Your time-series tables will automatically partition, compress old data, and handle millions of rows efficiently. ## One thought on “Setting Up TimescaleDB for Time Series Data with Postgres.app for all your “Time Scales”!”.

TimescaleDB provides fast, high-performance time-series analytics by extending PostgreSQL with hypertables, compression, and optimized queries for large datasets. TimescaleDB ensures consistent, reliable data ingestion for real-time monitoring, analytics, and dashboarding. TimescaleDB enables fast, reliable, and scalable time-series analytics on all supported platforms, giving you the freedom to store, query, and analyze data efficiently anytime, anywhere. Always download and install TimescaleDB from the official website or trusted repositories to ensure security, stability, and compatibility for real-time data workloads. TimescaleDB is a PostgreSQL extension that enables high-performance storage, querying, and analytics of time-series and event data efficiently.

results.title: How TimescaleDB helped us scale analytics and reporting
results.snippet: We’ve seen up to 100% improvement in SELECT query performance when we simply changed the order of account\_id and device\_id in our multicolumn index. That’s when we started to see performance degradation — particularly for large customers querying 7+ day time ranges across tens of thousands of devices. By aggregating this data ahead of time, we can collapse millions of rows into a few hundred per interval and query dramatically smaller, narrower tables. What was particularly interesting for us was range-partitioning on the timestamp column, because our customers wanted longer data retention, up to one year, and storing one year of data in one large table would have destroyed query performance. **Columnstore performance for real-time analytics:** TimescaleDB’s hybrid row/columnar engine, Hypercore, enables fast scans and aggregations over large datasets. For many teams at Cloudflare, TimescaleDB strikes a phenomenal balance between the simplicity of storing your analytical data under the same roof as your configuration data, while also gaining much of the impressive performance of a specialized OLAP system.
